summary: A 1987 Stephen Sondheim musical (lyrics and music) with a book by James Lapine, their second collaboration. Interweaves fairy tale characters — Cinderella, Jack (and the Beanstalk), Little Red Riding Hood, Rapunzel, and the Baker and his Wife — who venture into the woods to fulfill their wishes. The Witch drives the plot by demanding the Baker collect four items (a cow as white as milk, a cape as red as blood, hair as yellow as corn, a slipper as pure as gold) to reverse a curse. Act II subverts fairy-tale endings as characters face consequences. Joanna Gleason originated the role of the Baker's Wife. Bernadette Peters originated the Witch. The musical opens and closes with the word 'I wish.'

works.name: Songs: "Agony"
works.description: A comedic duet performed by Cinderella's Prince and Rapunzel's Prince, in which they try to one-up each other over their respective loves. The two princes compete about whose romantic suffering is greater, singing about "what's intriguing / or half so fatiguing / as what's out of reach." One of the most frequently clued songs from the show. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Two princes perform this song while trying to one-up each other's love for Cinderella and Rapunzel
examples: Two princes in this musical perform the song 'Agony' while trying to one-up each other's love for Cinderella and Rapunzel
It includes the songs 'Agony,' 'It Takes Two,' and 'No One is Alone.'

works.name: Songs: "No One is Alone"
works.description: A consoling ensemble number sung near the end of the musical, after several characters have died. Cinderella reassures Little Red Riding Hood, Jack, and the Baker that they are not alone. One of the most referenced songs from the show in quizbowl. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Cinderella consoles characters by singing this song after the deaths of Granny and the Baker's Wife
examples: This character consoles characters in another musical by singing 'No One is Alone' after the deaths of Granny and the Baker's Wife.
It includes the songs 'Agony,' 'It Takes Two,' and 'No One is Alone.'

works.name: Songs: "On the Steps of the Palace"
works.description: Cinderella's solo number in which she deliberates about her 'first big decision' — whether to leave her slipper on the steps of the palace or flee. The prince has spread sticky pitch on the stairs to trap her. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Cinderella sings about her 'first big decision' in abandoning an object (her slipper) on some steps

A large amount of sticky pitch spread over a set of stairs immobilizes this character's escape, prompting her to sing 'On the Steps of the Palace'

works.name: Songs: "Stay with Me"
works.description: The Witch's plea to Rapunzel, her adopted daughter, urging her not to leave. The Witch sings 'Stay with me / The world is dark and wild. / Stay a child while you can be a child.' Wikipedia

clues.clue: The Witch implores her adopted daughter (Rapunzel): 'Stay with me / The world is dark and wild. / Stay a child while you can be a child.'

works.name: Songs: "Any Moment"
works.description: A duet between Cinderella's Prince and the Baker's Wife, during which the Baker's Wife briefly entertains an affair. Shortly after, the Baker's Wife is crushed by a giant's foot. Wikipedia

clues.clue: After cheating on her husband during this song, the Baker's Wife is crushed by a foot

works.name: Characters: The Baker and Baker's Wife
works.description: A married couple inspired by the Rapunzel fairy tale who desperately want a child. The Witch tasks them with collecting four items — a cow as white as milk, a cape as red as blood, hair as yellow as corn, and a slipper as pure as gold — to reverse a curse placed on their family. Joanna Gleason originated the role of the Baker's Wife on Broadway. The Baker's Wife dies in Act II, crushed by a giant. Wikipedia

works.name: Characters: The Witch
works.description: The central antagonist who drives the plot by placing a curse on the Baker's family and demanding four items to reverse it. She needs a cow as white as milk, a cape as red as blood, hair as yellow as corn, and a slipper as pure as gold. She is Rapunzel's adoptive mother. Bernadette Peters originated the role. Wikipedia

works.name: Characters: Cinderella
works.description: Cinderella's storyline follows the Brothers Grimm version. She calls birds to help pick lentils from ashes so she can attend the King's Festival. The Prince spreads sticky pitch on the palace steps to catch her. In Act II, she breaks up with the Prince over his infidelity and helps raise Little Red and Jack with the Baker. Cinderella opens and closes the show by singing 'I wish.' Anna Kendrick played her in the 2014 film. Wikipedia

works.name: Characters: Jack
works.description: Based on Jack and the Beanstalk. Jack sells his cow Milky White for magic beans. He feeds Milky White golden cornsilk to fulfill the prophecy about 'hair as yellow as corn.' He climbs the beanstalk and meets 'big, tall, terrible, awesome, scary' beings in the sky, stealing gold coins and a gold egg. Wikipedia

works.name: Characters: The Narrator / The Giant
works.description: In the stage version, the show includes a Narrator character who is sacrificed to a giant in Act II. The giant is a major antagonist in the second act, crushing the Baker's Wife underfoot. Wikipedia

clues.clue: In the stage version, the narrator is sacrificed to a giant

works.name: Musical Motifs and Structure
works.description: The musical features distinctive leitmotifs. A recurring motif begins with a descending fifth (A down to D, E, C-sharp, B) associated with the warning 'Don't you know what's out there in the world?' The motif 'short D, long E' represents the closing words 'I wish.' The show was Sondheim's second collaboration with librettist James Lapine (after Sunday in the Park with George). Wikipedia

clues.clue: A motif begins with a descending fifth of A down to D, E, C-sharp, B — associated with 'Don't you know what's out there in the world?'
examples: A motif in this musical begins with a descending fifth of 'A, down to D, E, C-sharp, B' and occurs as a character warns, 'Don't you know what's out there in the world?'

works.name: The Magic Beans
works.description: Five magic beans are traded for Milky White, the cow. The beans, when planted, grow a giant beanstalk. In the 2014 film, they burst in flashes of orange when they hit the ground. The Witch drinks a potion made from Milky White's milk to regain her beauty. A dedicated tossup has been written specifically about the magic beans as a standalone answer. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Five beans were traded for Milky White, a cow whose milk produces a potion the Witch drinks to regain her beauty

works.name: General / Identification
works.description: How Into the Woods is typically identified in giveaway clues: a fairytale-inspired 1987 musical by Stephen Sondheim, interweaving Jack and the Beanstalk, Little Red Riding Hood, Cinderella, and Rapunzel. Often paired with Sweeney Todd and Company as Sondheim identifiers. Bernadette Peters frequently collaborated with Sondheim. Wikipedia

comprehensive_summary:
Into the Woods is one of the most frequently referenced Stephen Sondheim musicals in quizbowl, appearing both as a direct answerline and as one of the standard identifiers for Sondheim (alongside Sweeney Todd, Company, and Sunday in the Park with George). The musical premiered in 1987 with a book by James Lapine, making it their second collaboration after Sunday in the Park with George.

The most commonly clued elements are the songs "Agony" (the two princes' duet), "No One is Alone" (Cinderella's consolation number), and "On the Steps of the Palace" (Cinderella's deliberation about leaving her slipper). The Baker and Baker's Wife are central characters, with Joanna Gleason's Tony-winning origination of the Baker's Wife being a frequent clue. The Witch's four-item curse (cow as white as milk, cape as red as blood, hair as yellow as corn, slipper as pure as gold) appears regularly.

Harder clues focus on musical motifs — particularly the descending fifth motif (A-D-E-C#-B) and the "short D, long E" motif representing "I wish." The narrator's sacrifice to the giant is a stage-specific detail that appears at higher difficulties. The magic beans have been the subject of their own standalone tossup. Cinderella is the character most frequently asked about in connection with this show, particularly her opening/closing "I wish" and her Act II decision to leave the Prince.

In mention-based clues, Into the Woods most often appears as a Sondheim identifier, typically listed with Sweeney Todd. Bernadette Peters and the 2014 film adaptation (with Anna Kendrick as Cinderella) also generate mentions.
